entry.title: Re: 🎉SAP Build Brings Generative AI to Process Automation to enable your Business Processes
entry.content: Thanks for the tutorial @yogananda! 👍🏻

entry.title: Event Based Production and WIP reversal
entry.content:
We have customer scenario in Event-based WIP. The customer wants to reverse the Material before the teco in case there has been some specification changes or for any reason. In event-based once we do the GR the WIP is closed and we are not able to see that back . since the manufacturing cycle is longer they would want to have a view of the WIP and also as a part of financial reporting they require this.

Is there any way we can reverse the material after GR to WIP back. 

than you

entry.title: Integration Center not fetching records - RCM Background check
entry.content:
Hi

We have set up the standard third party integration with our background check vendor.The background check runs successfully but integration center job keeps saying "No Records found for integration; job finished without generating any output " and candidates do not exit SF  to land up on the vendor dashboard.

What does the message indicate?Is there some config missing or is this a problem on the vendor's side?

Please can you help here.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content: Thats a great feature, but I dont see it in the JMS adapter in my Tenant, any pointers there ?

entry.title: MRP run not to consider batches with expiry date
entry.content:
Dear team

We want to run MRP for 6 months and we do not want MRP to consider batches which are going to expire in 6 months in future.

 

BR

Ramesh Kumar

entry.title: Calculating Time Interval between Two Time Feilds
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere #TimeInterval

Hi All,
I have requirement to calculate the time interval between two Time Feilds in Graphical View using Calculated Column
There are 4 Dimension for a requirement with data in below format. I need to calculate time interval between Process Order Start Time and Finish Time in Hours. I have tried various function but it's not giving accurate results. Can anyone help me to calculate the expected results.

Process Order Actual Start DateProcess Order Actual Finish DateProcess Order Actual Start Time (HHMISS)Process Order Actual Finish Time (HHMISS)Time Interval between Process Order Start Time and Finish Time in hrs (Expected Result)
20250106202501071641440000008
202501072025012206393300000018
20250117
20250117
000000
093135
9

entry.title: Registration For SAP Sapphire Virtual In 2025 Is Now Live!
entry.content:
It’s that time of year again! This year’s SAP Sapphire Virtual is taking place on May 20-21, 2025, and you can be there to discover how SAP helps businesses go beyond the AI hype to bring out real results. Our annual two-day flagship interactive event is accessible to everyone. 

Here’s what you can expect

SAP Sapphire is all about SAP's strategic vision, innovations, and industry-leading solutions in action. By joining the virtual experience, you can gain the insights and expertise needed to advance your career from the comfort of home, and help your organization thrive using SAP technologies. Get ready to learn from industry leaders, discover best practices from SAP experts and partners and explore the latest technology shaping the future of business. 

  • Discover how SAP Business Suite helps realize your full potential 
  • Experience real stories about SAP driving digital transformation 
  • Connect with our global community to learn and share strategies 

The packed agenda includes access to live content from the SAP Sapphire show floor in Orlando, including livestreamed keynotes, exclusive interviews, and interactive sessions, and on-demand content available at any time.  

Choose your Path: Three SAP Sapphire Virtual tracks to choose from 

At SAP Sapphire Virtual, you can dive deep into three event tracks providing numerous sessions per track, easy navigation and alignment to in-person tracks, each offering a variety of sessions and formats:  

  • Business Applications 
  • Business Technologies and  
  • Transformation and Adoption 

Level up with learning and SAP Certification  

Stay tuned to learn more about how registrants can access and benefit from the SAP Sapphire Virtual learning zone with curated learning content and experiences, as well as access to a community of experts. Get ready to acquire in-demand skills and prepare for a globally recognized SAP Certification to boost your career.  

Register now for SAP Sapphire Virtual 2025 and shape the future of business 

Register now for our premier flagship event to explore SAP's strategic vision, groundbreaking innovations, and industry-leading solutions in action. Plus, you can take advantage of an exclusive package of curated learning, engagement, and solution experience offerings—all designed to help you stay ahead in today's fast-moving business landscape.​ Join business leaders and decision-makers at SAP Sapphire Virtual in 2025 and stay tuned for an immersive learning experience to expand your expertise and accelerate your career.​ 
 
See you all at SAP Sapphire Virtual!

entry.title: Re: Report Utilization
entry.content:
Hi @Melissa_S , hope you're doing great.

Please check below KBA, which brings an overview about the possibilities of Report Usage through the different types of reports available in SuccessFactors:

2499914 - Usage statistics / Audit log for Reports

Best Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: MM_PUR_S4_PR~MODIFY_ITEM Contract for limit missing in importing parameters
entry.content: Hi there --- I hate to ask, but why do you need to do this enhancement in the first place? What are the purchase requisitions being created for? Do they have a material master number? I'm just wondering if you could solve all of this by implementing source lists as opposed to doing the enhancement. I'm not much help on ABAP though ! Thanks!

entry.title: SEN on prem fails create project with "now write permission"
entry.content:
Hello,

We run SEN on prem (older version).

1 producer user goes to create a project in a group.  goes to group and right clicks, chooses new project.
They get an error message that starts with "no write permission"

backstory, seems their password had expired, went to manager, reset the password, checked, they are standard author there, have worked in the workarea before.

notified them of the password, they connected to manager in producer, did a synch.

still same error.

Asked them to reboot workstation, retry, same error.

Any thoughts/items for me to check?

Best Regards, Wallace

SAP Enable Now, author option  SAP Enable Now

entry.title: test
Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
@ArjenVHooydonk Thanks for your comment. We have a phased roll-out of new Dcs happening this year as of now (March 19th), the DCs from US, Switzerland, and Australia are available. Germany is planned for the end of March (remaining roll-outs planned for this year). In your case (which we already discussed), you had a system in the DC from Germany which will be re-provisioned to the new stack as soon as the DC is ready. 

And regarding your other comment about "comparison of documentation", we will take that feedback to our teams and check internally if this can be improved.

entry.title: Re: First look into SAP Datasphere's new version control functionality
entry.content: Thanks Simon !

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
@MatthiasL Same answer as above. We have a phased roll-out of new Dcs happening this year as of now (March 19th), the DCs from US, Switzerland, and Australia are available. Germany is planned for the end of March (remaining roll-outs planned for this year). 

It is part of our plan to start re-provisioning systems that are not yet used. We will start this in Q2 2025 and will proceed based on the DC availability. If you have further questions, please do reach out to us.

entry.title: The order quantity should not be confirmed after the sales order is saved.
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am getting an issue at after saving the sales order confirmed quantity should not picked.

At the time of creating the sales order, the confirmed quantity should be allocated for this order. In the below screenshot could you refer to it?

But, after saving the sales order, the confirmed quantity should be showing zero value, Please suggest any solution for this issue.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: @MarcoSchramp Thanks for your understanding. As I mentioned above, the DC from Germany is targeted to be available around end of March, 2025.

entry.title: Re: In Integration Center, need help in filtering the pay...
entry.content:
Hi @niyatijain2 , hope you're fine.

Below is a logic that worked before, but was not tested recently.

Please check if you have tried to create your configuration like this, and then confirm if this suggestion can help you:

1 - Create a Calculated Field:

In your Integration Center job, add a new Calculated Field.
Name it "LowerCaseEmail" or something similar.
Use the following formula to convert the email to lowercase:
LOWER(email), Where email is the name of your original email field.

2 - Set up the Filter:

In the "Filters" section of your Integration Center job, create a new filter.
Use the following condition:
LowerCaseEmail ENDS_WITH '@gmail.com'

*LowerCaseEmail is the name of your calculated field from step 1.


3 - Apply the Filter:

Ensure the filter is activated for your job.
This should work restricting the data to only include records where the email (in lowercase) ends with '@gmail.com'.

****************

Regards,

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: @wilcoxt Thanks for your comment. We are in touch with all of our partners with previously approved add-ons and have so far listed on the new KBA 3553185, the ones that have been expressly declared as compatible with the new stack. In case of the add-on you mentioned, we are still waiting for the partner's input and once we have it, we will list it on the new KBA. If a partner does not declare compatibility, we will not be able to list such an add-on for the new stack.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: @Jan_Duthoo Thanks for your comment. Please reach out to us to discuss the details. We are currently aware of the KSA projects and are working with the involved teams.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Assignment of Sold-To Partner in STO Delivery (NL)
entry.content: Can you post what you see on partner functions of customer you assigned to you plant in T001W table ?-- as far i understand customer assigned in the STO settings will be the partner on delivery document --please share some screenshots of Delivery created via VL10B and manually you what value you entering and what you have in T001W table ?

entry.title: Generic Withholding Report
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is there somewhere that I can find what the generic withholding reports look like and what fields are included for LATAM countries?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Create condition table for tax code or tax procedure
entry.content:
if your requirement is to default the tax code on the invoice here few steps you can tax 

1. You can make use of info records if you are comfortable with it 

2. or you can default tax code based on a condition type or condition table (what parameters will define the tax code)-- to determine the tax code in the PO--here is an example and steps for achieving that requirement 

1. Create a condition table(999) with the Key Combination Plant/Vendor/Tax Code in t.code: M/03

2. Create an access sequence(ZTX1) in M/07 and assign condition table(999)

3. Create condition type ZTX1 ( a copy of MWST) and assign access sequence(ZTX1) in it(M/06).

4. Keep new Condition type ZTX1 maintained in the Pricing Procedure(M/08).

5. Now, maintain the Condition Record in t.code; MEK1 with Condition type ZTX1 and keep

your Plant/Vendor/Tax Code.

Now, when you create a Purchase Order with a combination of company code and plant, the tax code will be defaulted on the invoice tab.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Plan Delivery Time for Purchase requisition i...
entry.content:
Hello

I don't think you can influence the planned delivery time with variant configuration. 

BAdI MD_MODIFY_SOURCE allows you to use your own logic to determine the planned delivery time.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Payment term 001 not defined - Message No. F5839
entry.content:
Hi, 

You configured 0001 but error is for 001 -> this is different (001 NE 0001). Please configure 001.

entry.title: Re: Poland KSeF - Full Walk through
entry.content:
Hi, 

walk through You can fin in this note. 3279206 - Electronic Document Processing for Poland - KSeF: Implementation Overview

SAP solution is based on DRC.

entry.title: Tax code and Tax jurisdiction codes in ML81N
entry.content: We create service purchase orders with unplanned services and indicate the tax code and tax jurisdiction code on the purchase order.  When doing a service entry (ML81N) the tax codes/jurisdiction codes do not always populate the tax code/jurisdiction codes that are indicated in the purchase order.  Especially if there are multiple lines on the PO with differing tax codes/jurisdiction codes.  Is there a BADI or 'something' that would force the tax code/jurisdiction codes from the referenced PO/line item into the service entry sheet?  My Programmer can't seem to make it work...any assistance would be appreciated.!!

entry.title: test
Re: G/L Accounts not transported from DEV to TEST
entry.content:
Please check answer from this topic. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/transport-of-g-l-accounts-in-3sl/qaq-p/12593845

entry.title: Creating a CBO with CRUD operations | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction
Creating a Custom Business Object (CBO) in Public Cloud, from the initial setup to enabling CRUD operations. This includes communication scenario, arrangement, and catalog association. A simple process to enable customizations in the environment.


Step 1 - Creating the CBO (Custom Business Object)
Access the Custom Business Object app.



Create a new CBO.

 

 

 


To enable future CRUD access, select this option.
Add the required fields, define data types and keys, then click Publish.


Step 2 - Creating the Communication Scenario
Access the Custom Communication Scenarios app.


Create a new Communication Scenario.

 

 




Define a name and description.

Save and publish.





Step 3 - Creating the Communication Arrangement
Access the Communication Arrangements app.



Create a new Communication Arrangement.

 

 




Associate it with the previously created Communication Scenario.



Select the Communication System (e.g., ZLOCAL, already pointing to the environment).

 

 




Publish.


Step 4 - Linking Communications to the CBO
Open the previously created CBO.
In the Inbound Services tab, add the created service.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 



The Communication Arrangement is now associated.

 



Step 5 - Assigning the CBO to a Business Catalog
Access the Custom Catalog Extensions app. 



Locate the two objects that need to be linked to a Business Catalog (IBS and SCBO).

 






 

 



 

 




Publish.


Step 6 - Accessing the CBO CRUD (SM30 Equivalent)
1- Accessing through the CBO. Click in Go To Generated UI

 



2- Searching via apps.

 



Step 7 - Result
Click in Create to create a record.

entry.title: Re: Conditional Emails in Recruiting
entry.content:
No, you cannot have conditional emails based on questions. Only one email template can be associated with each applicant status. To achieve this, you would need two different applicant statuses one with an email and one without. You can then automate candidate movement using a business rule or integration.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
The Joule for developers capability you found first - Generation
From Joule's generation capability i can automate SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) code creation, accelerating development by generating entities, services, and Fiori elements. This enhances efficiency, reduces errors, and streamlines project setup.

entry.title: Re: Tables and Fields for Stock - Multiple Materials modu...

Hello -- please also could you give details on these fields:
Special Stock type
Material
Material Description

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud version 2 blank page
entry.content: Click Settings, make a user with more rights and lock this down as per the on-boarding guide.

entry.title: Releasing "requests" | In-App Mode (Custom Objects) | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In Public Cloud, the customization transport process differs from Private Cloud (STMS). The request is now called Collection. When transporting objects in In-App mode, each modification generates a new version within the Software Collection. You can add only the modified items, include comments, and use filters to streamline the search process. After importing the collection into the Test environment, performing the Forward action is necessary to make it available for import into PRD.

Step 1
Access the Export Software Collection app.



Create a Software Collection (if one does not already exist for the relevant topic or module/organization in the environment).


Name your Software Collection and click Create.

Once the Software Collection is created, note that it is versioned. This means that with every object modification and transport, a new version is automatically created for transport.




In Add Items, you can select the objects to be included in this Collection for transport.


Support Filters are available to assist with object searches within the environment.



After adding the desired objects, click Check to verify if there are any blockers, dependencies, or inconsistencies for transport.


Check Option
Note: It is possible to transport only the modified items or all items again. The first option is always recommended.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.


Export Option – This is where the release (Export) to the Test environment actually takes place.

 

A comment can be added for each versioned transport.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.

 


Step 2
After exporting, log in to the Test environment and access the Import Collection app.


Use the available filters to search for the desired collection. Upon reaching the Test environment, the versioned collection will have the status "Ready for Import."


Select the desired versioned collection and choose Check and Import.



Add a transport comment.



Click Manual Import.

The import process starts, and the collection status changes to "Importing" during processing.

 



 



Once the import is complete, a confirmation message is displayed:


Step 3
The collection has only been imported into the Test environment but is not yet available for import into the Production environment.

To make it available for import into PRD, it is necessary to perform the Forward action.

 


Select the collection and click Forward.

 



The collection is forwarded to the Production environment transport path.

 


To finalize, log in to the Production environment and access the Import Collection app.

 




Select the desired collection and import it.

 



The collection has been successfully imported into the PRD environment.

entry.title: Re: How to register an S/4RFQ to be linked to Sourcing
entry.content:
ME41 is still available in S4 but the S4 way to do is to use S4 Fiori Apps -

1, Manage RFQ--to create RFQ and Invite vendors (S4 creates only one document compared to each document with ME41 in ECC)

2. Once the Vendor has the response --You use Manage Supplier Quotations (instead of ME47)

3. You use the Compare Quotation app to compare vendors and award the quotation to the selected vendor 

4. Create a PO from the awarded quotation 

5. Close the RFQ once awarded 

Note -- S4 Manage RFQ does not support item category D-- you should be lean services if you are using service procurement or have use of getting RFQ or quote for item category D.

You can read more about RFQ -https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/f16d85d2491247a98c403654210adfd3.html

entry.title: How to Enable Joule Capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
SAP recently announced some new Joule capabilities for developers within SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps. If you’re wondering how to enable these capabilities in your workspace, you’re in the right place.

In this post, we’ll look at the steps to undertake to enable the AI capabilities. But first, let’s just recap the announcement and the various recent posts:

  • Joule for developers, announced on March 18, is a set of AI capabilities enabled by Joule across SAP Build and ABAP developer tools.
  • Read the announcement blog here
  • Read the technical blog here
  • Read the FAQs on Joule for developer here

Now, lets look at the steps to enable the AI capabilities in SAP Build:

1. On the Build homepage, click Control Tower in the left-hand navigation panel.

2. Select Generative AI from the settings canvas.

3. Toggle the Enable AI button

 

4. Read and accept the SAP AI terms

5. Verify that the AI features are enabled

Availability and Licensing

Joule capabilities for developers are currently available across SAP Build Code, ABAP, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps.

Good news! Now through the end of June, we’re making Joule capabilities for developers accessible for free to all developers using SAP Build and ABAP Development Tools. For all SAP Build development tools, this free period extends until the end of June 2025. The ABAP AI capabilities will be available for free until August 2025. The only requirement to take advantage of this free offer is for the organization/user to sign the SAP AI Terms. Once this free offer concludes, the commercial model based on AI Units will take effect.

Note: You will still need the license to use the underlying SAP Build solutions and ABAP to utilize the AI capabilities within those tools.

Start leveraging the power of AI today! Enable Joule for developers in your SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps environments and experience a significant boost in your development efficiency. Don't miss out on the free offer available for a limited time!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud version 2 blank page
entry.content: Hi, The onboarding guide does not contain any clear steps. Also, the only available option in the settings is "Users" and I have already created one, but it is not able to log in, showing an "Incorrect username or password" error.

entry.title: Re: Proxying as an associate
entry.content:
Hi @mdudley2 

As per KBA 3511497, currently the "Launch Proxy" action (Learning Administration > People > Users > Actions) will display the legacy Learning home page, even with the "New Learning Experience" toggle on for the user being proxied.

There have been some enhancements requests on it, and our customers are welcomed to vote for them and/or create additional requests:

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/322383
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/322521/?section=sectionDetails 

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

Also, follow the Learning news at SAP SuccessFactors What's New Viewer.

entry.title: Re: Envision the Future of Generative AI with SAP's AI Agents
entry.content: It's striking how difficult it can be to shift some clients away from their entrenched, customized business models, even when presenting clear explanations and diagrams of SAP's best practice processes. Now, to effectively promote SAP's new AI solutions, I suggest developing content that addresses the client's perspective, focusing on the human-psychological elements of change and how to effectively communicate the benefits of these solutions.

entry.title: SAP CIDS Certification / Study Material
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am an SAP IBP/PP Consultant. I would like to know about CPIDS or CIDS Material for certification. As i don't see any specific code for CIDS in Learning Hub what would be the alternative option to learn CIDS. As i mostly work on the SAP IBP integration topics with S4 Hana/ECC. Expectation is to learn the CIDS functionality from the beginning step by step. I am interested in the front end ( Extractors, Mapping, RFC's Source to Target systems) not on the Background technical connectivity or installation part.

Would be really great if some one can guide me to learn CIDS or to share some study Material.

Regards

Krish

entry.title: Re: Map Account Creation Fields at the Career Portal to C...
entry.content: The country code on the profile page will populate only if SMS integration is enabled. Otherwise, it will be stored only in the backend database and used solely for duplication checks. There is no way to display this standard field on the profile without enabling SMS integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud version 2 blank page
entry.content: Give it the role: https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SVC/973e02cfd684464a90afffdb608dab63/0d77977a86114f0188ad4987f8be907d.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Will there be an option to hide the toggle and default to the legacy workbench during 1H 2025 Releas
entry.content: We would like to hide the new applicant workbench and default to the legacy workbench or at least set the default to the legacy workbench.  Will they have the option to hide the toggle or to set the default for the workbench during the 1 H 2025 release?

entry.title: Report layout not being used in batch email
entry.content:
Hello all,  we have create a Z report as a copy of S_ALR_87012182.  This is because we needed to add fields and the ability to email. 

I created a layout and set it as the default.  I then created a variant with that layout and assigned it in the batch job.  

When we use the email option either upfront or in batch, the report is received but is not the layout in the variant.  In addition, the report in the email or in download had the field names as column headers rather than the description.  IE the column header says KUNNR instead of customer number.  

Is there something we're missing to get this report to email in the layout we want?  Is it maybe the way the ALV is set up?

Thank you

Cheryl Tackett

entry.title: Re: Functional Currency differs from Company Code Currency
entry.content:
Hi Venu,

Thank you for the reply. I have further question. 

I assume at the time of release it will use the valid exchange rate maintained in the system. For exchange rate differences, how does the system account for this in the material valuation? It could be at the time of release the exchange rate between HUF and USD, is for example 0.10, the standard cost at the time of release is 1000 HUF > 100 USD but after a month it became 0.23 with standard cost still @ 1000 HUF but USD value now is 230 USD. (HUF is local currency while USD is the functional currency) How does the system account for this exchange rate differences?

Regards, 

Derek

entry.title: Golden transactions in SAP S/4 Professional Services
entry.content:
Hi All,

We will soon be going live with S/4 Professional Services. We have a complex set of integrations going to & from SAP. Over the weekend prior to Go-Live, once the integrations are on, we are planning to run a set of transactions in Production to ensure the integration flows work as expected. We are currently thinking-thru the best approach for this i.e. postings on a dummy project OR identifying a live-project and making some (unreal) postings on it which can be reversed later.

Will be keen to you if other professional services firms would have done the same thing and if so, what approach was used to execute the tests?

 

Thanks,

Payal

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Viewing application profile from candidate's perspective
entry.content:
As an admin, you can control the permissions for fields displayed in the candidate profile and application through Manage Templates in the Admin Center.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: IDOC metadata change on SAP backend - Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on IDOC to IDOC scenario on BTP Cloud Integration(CI) where our receiver system is also another CI which is on another subaccount ...IDOC metadata has been changed on SAP backend.. we need to adapt the IDOC schema changes on Cloud Integration to synchronize the schema on both SAP Backend & CI.. should we ask SAP backend colleagues to share XSD (or) WSDL? which is better practice) if they share XSD/WSDL then should we also the share the same with receiver BTP CI system contact? 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Re: Resource not found for segment 'Purchaseorder'" in Pu...
entry.content:
Hi, 

please check this note 2743849 - Resource not found for the segment 'XXXXXXXX'

entry.title: Re: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @Shadab101,

The Deployment Scheduler supports CTS-managed transports only. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is not supported since those transports are managed by Adaptation Transport Organizer (ATO). The support of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is not planned for this year and I cannot communicate any time-plan yet.

Best regards,

Moritz

entry.title: Problems with the service layer in the Order POST
entry.content:
I use this endpoint
POST: http://xxxxxx:50003/b1s/v1/Orders
with this JSON

 

{

    "CardCode": "10260237-4C",

    "DocDate": "2025-03-19",

    "SalesPersonCode": -1,

    "Rounding": "tNO",

    "DocumentSubType": "bod_None",

    "Series": 13,

    "Comments": “Test",

    "DocumentLines": [

        {

            "ItemCode": "A00001",

            "PriceAfterVAT": 1785.0,

            "Quantity": 1.0,

            "TaxCode": "IVA",

            "WarehouseCode": "01"

        }

    ]

}

The response is :

{
    "error": {
        "code": -1,
        "message": {
            "lang": "en-us",
            "value": "BadFormat"
        }
    }
}
I changed the json to something simpler, but the error is the same.
{
    "CardCode": "10260237-4C",
    "DocDate": "2025-03-19",
    "DocumentLines": [
        {
            "ItemCode": "A00001",
            "Quantity": 1.0
        }
    ]
}
The product and business partner exist and are active
What's the problem? What am I missing?

Moritz

Web Preview works Fine GET, POST, PATCH

Hope experts can help me.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: @JACVancouver @SriniSingaraju  Will the recording of the Webinar on the March 18th be available?

entry.title: The NEW New Service Connection Process for Energy and Water
entry.content:
New service connection, a complex business
This process is a perfect example of integrating customer service, enterprise asset management and field service. Utilities have not changed how buildings are connected to the grid, however, work management operations and software solutions have improved significantly. Let me show you how the redesigned new service connection process runs with SAP service management, both from a customer and work management view. 

Reference process flow
If you compare a water connection for a single home with an electric connection for an office building, you can see both, a variation in complexity and a common pattern. We considered this to define a reference process with SAP service management. It has two main parts, a commercial phase followed by a technical phase. The main solutions are service and maintenance functions in S/4HANA Cloud private edition and Field Service Management.

Process steps

Steps 1 - 4, commercial phase:
First, during new construction, either a land developer, builder or contractor requests a new service connection. The process starts in customer service, online or with an agent. A service request is created and captures customer information, the new service location, dates and the type of request. Next is a follow-up service order for a field survey. A technician needs to assess, measure and plan the new connection design. With this information, the service agent can create a service quote and send it to the requestor.  When the quote has been accepted, the first phase, mostly commercial and customer centric, is complete.

Steps 5 - 8, technical execution phase:
In the second phase, a work manager receives the service order, plans the execution operations, equipment, material, schedules a crew and dispatches the work. A field crew performs the installation, time and material is recorded in the field, orders are confirmed. This phase, work and asset management centric, concludes when the orders are confirmed, billed and settled. The builder is informed that the connection is ready for meter installation.

Document flow
As you can tell by now, this is a long workflow, typically taking weeks. From a utility perspective, it is an 'ad-hoc service request' by an external party. That's why the backbone of this process is service with advanced execution, where service starts with a customer request and the execution is complex and linked to asset management. See also my other blog about this topic. 

Reference document flow new service connection process

What has changed and what is special?
Compared with previous solutions and releases, for example SAP ECC work management, customer service (CS), service management (PM/CS) or mobile solutions, many capabilities have changed. Let me point them out and add important remarks.

Service request and service order
The initial object is the (new) service request, followed by a (new) service order. I say (new) service to highlight the (new) service objects that became available in S/4HANA service with customer management. The service request is either created directly in S/4HANA, via an online form or from Service Cloud. Keep in mind, we do no longer use the sunset PM/CS objects, service notification PM-CS-SN and service orders PM-CS-SO.

This service request contains a customer/sold-to-party, dates, and we need a service loaction. If the functional location does not exist yet, it is created now or at least a building address is captured. Nothing new, just note that the new service objects can reference locations and equipment.

From here on there are about 10 subsequent process documents that follow the service request. They are connected via document flows, as shown in the picture above. This is very useful, copy control configuration and standard integrations automate the process.

Following the service request, the service order for the field survey uses a simple transaction type SRVO and a simple service product. I call it simple because we do not need any plant maintenance involved yet. This order is for a field technician to visit the construction site and gather information to plan the connection design. 

Field service management
The service order is dispatched to a surveyor in the field. Mind the service order to field service management (FSM) document flow, we utilize FSM rather than SAP Service and Asset Manager (SAM). The mobile FSM app uses smartforms to capture the connection specifics, for example 'residential electric service, 240V 2-phase, 200A, 15m long, above ground'. 

Service quotation
The service quotation document is key for the commercial agreement and for preparing the work management phase. The ability to quote service, including complex service with advanced execution, is new in S/4HANA. A quotation of the document type MSQ is a precursor of the service order with advanced execution of the type MSO. A different service product is used for the connection order item, now with an item category for advanced execution.

The calculation of the new connection amount payable by the builder is happening here, using item pricing. The utility can use simple or complex rules to determine a connection price. A debit request can be created for an upfront payment. 


Screen capture of a service quotation

Once the builder accepts the quotation, the maintenance service order is created automatically and visible in the transaction history document flow.

Service order with advanced execution
The service order with advanced execution of the type MSO (maintenance service order) links us to plant maintenance. Not much has changed in the linked PM order, see below, but watch the 'Service' section. This is new, it contains the link from PM back to service.  


Screen capture of a maintenance order

The maintenance order operations drive the field service activities. 


Screen capture of a maintenance order, operations


Field service management, again
FSM takes over with planning, dispatching and the field service execution. This is the heavy lifting of the service with advanced execution. Field work can include digging a trench, installing cables or pipes, setting poles and transformers, and mounting conduits and meter sockets. Field technicians record time, material, expenses and update equipment records in the mobile app.


Screen capture of the FSM dispatching board

You may have noticed, FSM works for both, simple customer centric service orders like the survey and here, for complex plant maintenance centric operations.

Completion
Once the order is technically complete, the new service, for example power or water, can be turned on. Because most services are metered, this starts the next process, a device installation in meter-to-cash. The confirmed orders are internally settled and billed. The new connection becomes a field asset, located at a customer address.

Summary
The redesigned service process is compatible with the current SAP solutions and suitable for most use cases, especially residential and commercial connections. The benefit of the new service objects is the flexibility and customer orientation. When needed for advanced execution, we use the extensive capabilities in plant maintenance. 

There are some exceptions to the applicability of this reference process. For example, a connection for an entire factory to the grid at a high voltage level would rather be seen as a capital project and managed like an investment.

entry.title: Need to connect to sybase db using sqlanywhere 17
entry.content: Need to connect to Sybase ASE 16 DB using SQLanywhere connector 16 for Powerbi

entry.title: Is it possible to use cross plant production for materials?
entry.content:
We would like to know if it is possible to use a cross plant scenario in production for materials in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to use cross plant production for mate...
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


Yes, in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud it is possible to use cross-plant production. In this scenario, a plant withdraws material from another plant or the production plant differs from the planning plant.

Please note that currently the system only supports cross-plant production in the same company code. For more information, kindly check below SAP Help Portal documentation:
Cross-Plant Production


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports runtime does not export barcode font...
entry.content: Did you copy over the Azalea UFL to the Server?

entry.title: Data Volume Management (DVM) configuration guide for SAP Solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

We are currently requested by Business to enable the Data Volume Management ( DVM ) through the SAP Solution Manager. We are currently following the SAP Note:

2716655 - How To Use the Data Volume Management APP - Step by Step guide .

As per the Note we can observe that the prerequisite for DVM is - 

Managed System supported: SAP S/4 PERSON_NAME , SAP Business Suite on PERSON_NAME ( only EHP7+ for SAP ERP 6.0 for the moment ), SAP BW/4 HANA

  • ST-PI 740 SP07 or higher
  • ST-A/PI 01S SP03 or higher

 

Our Current System is : 

SAP Solution Manager - 

  • SAP Solution Manager 7.2
  • Oracle Database 
  • SAP_BASIS 740 SP0021
  • ST_PI 740 SP0013
  • ST_API 01T_731 SP0003

 

SAP ECC Production System:

  • Database Version - Oracle
  • Operating System - Linux x86_64
  • Component Version - SAP ECC 6.0
  • SAP_BASIS -  731_SP0009
  • ST_PI - 2008_1_710 SP0038
  • ST_API - 01W_731 SP0001  

 

Could you please let us know if we can go ahead with these lower system properties ? If yes could you please share us a detailed step by step document or SAP Note to follow and implement DVM from Solution Manager for this managed system.

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data Volume Management (DVM) configuration guide for...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

From the details you provided regarding the managed system, this system does not meet the prerequisites to configure data collection for the DVM cloud APP. In this case I recommend to follow step by step configuration - Guided procedure authoring (GPA), via Tcode: SOLMAN_SETUP -> Data Volume Management scenario.

Please refer to this page : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/653345d394d8453baf5be81e6dda7565/85f1ca4301ae492da1c33fc4f8218b97.html?locale=en-US

Additional resources regarding Data Volume Management : 

  • Visit the Data Volume Management topic page on SAP Community
  • In the DVM Workcenter - (in Solution Manager) tile, click on User Guide - Data Volume Management User Guide -  to download the best practices document
  • The complete documentation of Data Volume Management in the SAP Help Portal 

 

Alternatively, please refer to the following Knowledge Based Article I have created: 2490056 - DVM configuration guide for SAP Solution Manager 7.2

entry.title: Re: Proxying as an associate
entry.content: Hi mdudley2, my mistake. I also have broader "Proxy" priveleges within SuccessFactors, not just LMS. Which is what my inital response refers to. You're quite right, regardless of the interface set by the user, the Proxy function in LMS displays the old version. You could consider turning off the "old" view altogether?

entry.title: Re: Printing a single page from multiple pages using Crys...
entry.content:
Hello,

OK now I see the new Windows 11 print dialog box, not sure why when I googled it the first time nothing was found...

I suggest moving back to the legacy Dialog boxes, I doubt R&D has done anything to use the new one. Therefore there is no way to set the values in code and you are limited to what the New Printer UI allows, it could be a bug in Microsoft's UI, you'll have to ask MS how to set values....

To set it back to the original see this https://www.winhelponline.com/blog/restore-legacy-print-dialog-windows-11/

FYI - Using the CR Basic to print reports doesn't work that well to make changes to the printer saved in the RPT File. I suggest you have a look at my printer test app to use the PrintOutputController() to set printer info, it does require using RAS to open the report object. My sample shows you how to do this.

I will ping R&D and see what they say about it and if there are any plans to integrate it into CR's Printer dialog boxes.

Sorry about not seeing this sooner...

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Restricted measure not having opt...
entry.content:
Things to consider... 

 

The issue you're encountering with Fiscal Dates in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) after reloading your planning model is likely related to the date dimension properties and how SAC interprets Fiscal Dates versus standard dates.

Possible Causes and Solutions:

1. Date Dimension Type and Format Issue

In SAC, Fiscal Dates often behave differently from standard dates because they are linked to fiscal year variants.

If the system does not recognize them as a time-based dimension in the expected way, you may not get the Date Range selection option.


Solution:

Navigate to Modeler > Time Dimension Settings and check if the Fiscal Dates are correctly assigned to the time hierarchy.

Ensure the Fiscal Date dimensions are correctly mapped to a time hierarchy in the model.


2. Restricted Measure Behavior on Fiscal Dates

When creating a Restricted Measure or Account, Date Range selection is only available for recognized Date Dimensions.

If the Fiscal Dates are custom fields or incorrectly classified, SAC may not recognize them as date-based dimensions.


Solution:

Go to the model and check if the Fiscal Dates are correctly marked as Date Dimensions (under Model Properties > Time Dimensions).

Try reloading the model metadata after reloading the data to refresh the time structure.


3. Model Reload May Have Removed Time Hierarchy

When you reloaded the planning model, the assigned Fiscal Calendar or Time Hierarchy settings might have been altered.

In the model, check if the Fiscal Date fields still have the appropriate Fiscal Year Variant settings.

If the hierarchy is missing, reassign the time hierarchy in the model.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content:
CODECOMPLETION

Code completion is one of the most important skills because it speeds up development, reduces errors, ensures the correct use of standard functions, and most importantly, helps maintain consistency by following current coding guidelines.

#SAP #Joule #AIML #ABAPCloud #SAPBuild #BAS #ProcessAutomation#ChatBot #Capability

entry.title: Eligibility Rule for Selecting Benefits (only 1 benefits)
entry.content:
How do I create a rule to only assign a specific benefit to an employee if he or she has not been enrolled in another benefit?

I have 2 benefits available, but if he or she opts for one, he or she will not be able to enroll in the other.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to Enable Joule Capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps

I found the post, here is the link to review known limitations and available regions:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-brings-generative-ai-to-process-automation-to-enable-your/ba-p/14049869

entry.title: Re: Proxying as an associate
entry.content: @mdudley2, on top of what @M_jess1 mentioned, it's currently targeted to have it improved with the 1H2025 release (User proxy for administrators through the new homepage). Additional details are to be available in the What's New Viewer in the upcoming weeks.

entry.title: Re: Issues with PostgreSQL Connection in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
When CR connects it queries the DB Server for all of the PS, Views, Tables etc.

It can run out of space to load all of the info.

To add filtering right click on the Connection and select Options:

Uncheck all but one of the Data Explorer Options and see if you can see them now. If try adding filtering in the Like% options to see if that helps.

FYI, 16 is not currently supported:

PostgreSQLPostgreSQL (21)9.6ODBC  PostgreSQL 9.6 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.1 JDBC End of Support
10ODBC  PostgreSQL 10 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
11ODBC NewPostgreSQL 11 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC  NewPostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
12ODBC BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 12 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct
13ODBC BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct
14ODBC BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct
15ODBC BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct

entry.title: Adobe Forms - XML version comparison tool
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In this blog I'll present to you an alternative way to compare Adobe Form layout XML versions.

Introduction

As you may know, comparing Adobe Forms layout XML versions can be a time-consuming and cumbersome task.

For simpler objects such as programs/includes/classes/etc we can always rely on the split-screen version comparison tool:


However, it's not quite simple when it comes to compare Adobe Form XML versions.
You'd have to browse into each version and download its XML Source, and then compare it using an external editor / comparison tool:


In order to facilitate/streamline that, I've built a tool that can do all that with more ease, very similar to what we can do with programs/includes/classes/etc.

Solution

In short, in this new program:

  1. You enter a form name
  2. Choose the two versions you want to compare and press ‘Compare XML’



Source code:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report YJPEREZ_AFORM_COMPARE_XML_VERS
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT yjperez_aform_compare_xml_vers.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 2000.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 2000.

PARAMETERS: p_aform TYPE fpcontextt-name.

CLASS lcl_event_handler DEFINITION.

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS
      user_command FOR EVENT after_user_command OF cl_gui_alv_grid IMPORTING e_ucomm.
    CLASS-METHODS
      toolbar FOR EVENT toolbar OF cl_gui_alv_grid IMPORTING e_object.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    CONSTANTS c_ucomm_compare_xml TYPE sy-ucomm VALUE 'CMP_XML'.

ENDCLASS.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

  DATA lt_excluded_sel_functions TYPE TABLE OF rsexfcode.
  lt_excluded_sel_functions = VALUE #(
    ( fcode = 'GET' )
    ( fcode = 'VSHO' )
    ( fcode = 'VDEL' )
    ( fcode = 'SPOS' )
  ).

  CALL FUNCTION 'RS_SET_SELSCREEN_STATUS'
    EXPORTING
      p_status  = sy-pfkey
    TABLES
      p_exclude = lt_excluded_sel_functions.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  TRY.

      DATA(lv_objtype_adobe_form) = CONV vrsd-objtype( 'SFPF' ).

      DATA(lo_vers_alv) = NEW cl_vers_objtype_ui_alv(
        iv_objname = |{ p_aform }|
        iv_objtype = lv_objtype_adobe_form
      ).

      lo_vers_alv->get_vrs_for_display(
        IMPORTING
          et_vrs_disp       = DATA(lt_vers_data)
      ).

      TYPES:
        BEGIN OF ts_simple_vers_data,
          status   TYPE vrs_disp-status,
          versno   TYPE vrs_disp-versno,
          versnum  TYPE vrs_disp-versnum,
          rel      TYPE vrs_disp-rel,
          korrno   TYPE vrs_disp-korrno,
          korrtext TYPE vrs_disp-korrtext,
          project  TYPE vrs_disp-project,
          date     TYPE vrs_disp-date,
          time     TYPE vrs_disp-time,
          usr      TYPE vrs_disp-usr,
        END OF ts_simple_vers_data.

      DATA lt_simple_vers_data TYPE TABLE OF ts_simple_vers_data.

      DATA(lo_grid) = NEW cl_gui_alv_grid(
        i_parent      = cl_gui_container=>screen0
        i_appl_events = abap_true
      ).

      DATA(ls_grid_layout) = VALUE lvc_s_layo(
        sel_mode = if_dmc_ui_view_list_builder=>co_selection_mode_multiple
      ).

      DATA(lo_simple_vers_data_descr) = CAST cl_abap_tabledescr( cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data(
        p_data      = lt_simple_vers_data
      ) ).

      DATA(lo_simple_vers_data_line_descr) = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( lo_simple_vers_data_descr->get_table_line_type( ) ).

      DATA(lt_field_cat) = VALUE lvc_t_fcat( ).

      DATA(lt_components) = lo_simple_vers_data_line_descr->get_components( ).
      LOOP AT lt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).

        DATA(lo_elemdescr) = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( ls_component-type ).
        DATA(ls_ddic) = lo_elemdescr->get_ddic_field( ).

        APPEND VALUE #(
          fieldname = ls_component-name
          scrtext_s = ls_ddic-scrtext_s
          scrtext_m = ls_ddic-scrtext_m
          scrtext_l = ls_ddic-scrtext_l
          col_opt   = abap_true
        ) TO lt_field_cat.

      ENDLOOP.

      lt_simple_vers_data = CORRESPONDING #( lt_vers_data ).

      lo_grid->set_table_for_first_display(
        EXPORTING
          is_layout             = ls_grid_layout
        CHANGING
          it_fieldcatalog       = lt_field_cat
          it_outtab             = lt_simple_vers_data
      ).

      SET HANDLER lcl_event_handler=>toolbar FOR lo_grid.
      SET HANDLER lcl_event_handler=>user_command FOR lo_grid.

      lo_grid->set_ready_for_input( 1 ).

      CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 2000.

    CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).

      MESSAGE lx_root->get_longtext( ) TYPE 'I'.

  ENDTRY.

CLASS lcl_event_handler IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD toolbar.

    CLEAR: e_object->mt_toolbar.

    APPEND VALUE #(
      butn_type = if_sat_ui_button_types=>normal
      function = c_ucomm_compare_xml
      icon = '@46@'
      text = 'Compare XML'
    ) TO e_object->mt_toolbar.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD user_command.

    CASE e_ucomm.

      WHEN c_ucomm_compare_xml .

        lo_grid->get_selected_rows(
          IMPORTING
            et_index_rows = DATA(lt_index_rows)
        ).

        IF lines( lt_index_rows ) = 2.

          DATA(ls_left_version) = lt_simple_vers_data[ lt_index_rows[ 1 ] ].
          DATA(lo_left_form) = cl_fp_wb_helper=>form_create_from_version(
            i_name = p_aform
            i_version = CONV #( ls_left_version-versno )
          ).
          DATA(lo_left_form_obj) = CAST cl_fp_form( lo_left_form->get_object( ) ).
          DATA(lo_left_form_lay) = lo_left_form_obj->if_fp_form~get_layout( ).
          DATA(lv_left_version_xstr) = lo_left_form_lay->get_layout_data( ).
          DATA(lv_left_version_str) = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_string(
            iv_xstr   = lv_left_version_xstr
            iv_cp     = cl_ehprc_c_gen_cp=>code_page_latin1
          ).

          SPLIT lv_left_version_str AT cl_abap_char_utilities=>cr_lf INTO TABLE DATA(lt_left_version_str).

          DATA(ls_right_version) = lt_simple_vers_data[ lt_index_rows[ 2 ] ].
          DATA(lo_right_form) = cl_fp_wb_helper=>form_create_from_version(
            i_name = p_aform
            i_version = CONV #( ls_right_version-versno )
          ).
          DATA(lo_right_form_obj) = CAST cl_fp_form( lo_right_form->get_object( ) ).
          DATA(lo_right_form_lay) = lo_right_form_obj->if_fp_form~get_layout( ).
          DATA(lv_right_version_xstr) = lo_right_form_lay->get_layout_data( ).
          DATA(lv_right_version_str) = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_string(
            iv_xstr   = lv_right_version_xstr
            iv_cp     = cl_ehprc_c_gen_cp=>code_page_latin1
          ).

          SPLIT lv_right_version_str AT cl_abap_char_utilities=>cr_lf INTO TABLE DATA(lt_right_version_str).

          lo_grid->set_visible( abap_false ).

          DATA(ls_transport_key) = VALUE trkey(
            obj_type = lv_objtype_adobe_form
            obj_name = p_aform
            sub_type = lv_objtype_adobe_form
            sub_name = p_aform
          ).

          DATA(lv_title_left) = |{ ls_left_version-versno } ({ ls_left_version-date DATE = ENVIRONMENT } { ls_left_version-time TIME = ENVIRONMENT })|.
          DATA(lv_title_right) = |{ ls_right_version-versno } ({ ls_right_version-date DATE = ENVIRONMENT } { ls_right_version-time TIME = ENVIRONMENT })|.

          DATA(lt_source_l) = CONV rswsourcet( lt_left_version_str ).
          DATA(lt_source_r) = CONV rswsourcet( lt_right_version_str ).
          CALL FUNCTION 'WB_SPLIT_EDITOR_START'
            EXPORTING
              mode_left         = swbm_c_op_display
              mode_right        = swbm_c_op_display
              object_left       = CONV syrepid( p_aform )
              object_right      = CONV syrepid( p_aform )
              title_left        = lv_title_left
              title_right       = lv_title_right
              transport_key_l   = ls_transport_key
            TABLES
              source_l          = lt_source_l
              source_r          = lt_source_r                 " ABAP Source
            EXCEPTIONS
              parameter_invalid = 1                " Invalid parameter
              source_not_found  = 2
              not_executed      = 3
              error_message     = 4
              OTHERS            = 5.

          lo_grid->set_visible( abap_true ).

        ELSE.

          MESSAGE i103(sb).

        ENDIF.

    ENDCASE.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Feel free to try this tool and share any suggestions/insights if you have them 🙂


Conclusion


To sum up, this kind of Adobe Form XML comparisons are very clunky and impractical by nature.
Nevertheless, by doing it with such a program we can easily make such comparisons with much less effort.



Any questions/comments/suggestions?
Feel free to leave them down below.

For more resources related to Adobe Forms, feel free to:
– Follow the Adobe Form topic
– Post/answer/check questions related to Adobe Forms


Best regards,
Joaquim Perez

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers FAQ: Features, Access and More
entry.content:
@kushalsambhunath_banerjee : Yes, while Joule for Developers (this blog) may not directly help you, Joule for Consultants should when it is released later this year:

https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-assistant/sap-consulting-capability.html

entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector 2.18 is out!
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the availability of SAP Cloud Connector version 2.18. Building on previous iterations, this latest version offers several technical enhancements and solutions aimed at streamlining operations. Here are the key updates:

This release introduces support for Windows Server 2025. If you're preparing to upgrade your infrastructure, now’s the time to explore this compatibility. Note that some older OS versions will no longer be supported, so check the Product Availability Matrix for precise details.

Certificates are crucial for maintaining secure connections, but they come with expiration dates. As frequently requested by our users, the new version supports automatic renewal for subaccounts, minimizing the risk of downtime due to forgotten refreshes.

Don’t forget to enable Certificate refresh in the BTP Cockpit. This capability isn't available in all cloud regions immediately; it will be rolled out in phases across different regions. Once it becomes available in your region, the toggle button in the BTP Cockpit will be visible.

The administration UI defaults to Morning Horizon theme. However, you can also choose from other Horizon options, including Evening Horizon and Black/White High Contrast variants.

Navigate to Configuration > User Interface to set your preferred theme.

Version 2.18 introduces centralized management of E-mail and LDAP server trust stores directly within the Cloud Connector, bypassing the traditional JDK trust store. This setup allows finer control over securing backend communications with trusted certificates.

A new Troubleshooting section proactively diagnoses problems within the Cloud Connector.

 You’ll find detailed reports on issues, especially those affecting data tunnels, along with root cause analyses and potential fixes. The framework categorizes and tracks recurring issues, providing insights into their recurrence patterns over time.

The cross-subaccount performance monitor aggregates metrics from all subaccounts related to cloud-to-on-premises traffic. 

This consolidation aids in a more comprehensive analysis, helping you optimize resource allocation and network configurations efficiently.

Configure a fadeout time for service channels in HA setups to ensure smoother transitions when roles change intentionally between master and shadow instances. Known as the Service Channel Termination Grace Period, this new feature minimizes service disruptions by maintaining active channels during switches.

Certificate-based authentication now has a fallback mechanism to revert to user/password authentication if necessary. This added flexibility in logon processes ensures continual access, even when certificate-related issues arise.

Overall, SAP Cloud Connector 2.18 delivers enhancements focused on facilitating better security management, improving performance insights, and increasing reliability in high-stakes environments. Upgrade today to leverage these new features. For all technical details, see our comprehensive release notes.

Happy connecting!

entry.title: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey - Interlude
entry.content:
Ok, in this one I am going to do one simple trick: I am going to write the first part now, and then second one in ~6 months later after I will have finished my current project (s/4 private cloud implementation).

First part will be something of "product pitch" based on my naive understanding (these 2 months) of what is going on in cpi world in terms of dev tools and all that stuff.

The second one (lets call it "reality response") probably will thrash most of those thoughts/ideas, but it could also be a good read to someone stumbling upon this series in some years to come (yes, one of those 10 persons reading it in 2030, I did it for you!)

Pitch part (March'25)

We will use very basic "pitch deck" format:

  1. Problem
  2. Solution
  3. Demo
  4. Market Development Potential
  5. Business model
  6. Team / Advantage
Problem statement

Lack/absense of pro-code tools allowing local development / testing / debugging of iflows (camel routes)

In more details:

  • Having all artifacts in vcs (for paralel development, code review etc)
  • And therefore being able to use other local dev tools for certain artifacts (xslt, groovy scripts etc)
  • Being able to do local testing / debugging of iflows in local karaf instance (we need those jars for karaf published somewhere at tools.hana.ondemand.com)
  • Being able to run automated tests (for iflows that are designed testable, of course)
Solution

Open-source tooling available free of charge, supported by both sap and community.

For now (based on problem definition) let's assume that CI/CD is out of scope, but complete suite must definitely include this (seems like we currently miss that part anyway, while the same approach we use for cap ci/cd - simply running mbt in pipelines and then deploying mtars to CF - could probably work here as well).

Demo

Well, basically, this is why I did all those demo videos in my series.

But if I were to pick one, the last part would be my choice.

Just in case, let's briefly mention features that were demoed:

  • setup of local karaf instance through ui (almost completely)
  • manual sync (rezip) of locally stored artifacts to cpi
  • manual sync to local git repo back from cpi
  • ui for manual adaptation of camel blueprints to run locally (this is a critical gap on our path to proper automated testing)
  • ui for cpi endpoints testing (primitive http client)
  • ui for local testing of iflows and remote replay of messages on cpi side
Market Development Potential

I mean, seriously, it's sap cosmos.. One good - and especially free / opensource - tool dominates the market.

Then monetisation comes in forms of consulting and proper execution of projects utilising the tool.

Let's instead focus on what could be added later into product:

  • Fully local development of iflows (theoretically, we just need that bpmn to camel translator they use)
  • Proper BTP CI/CD that we already mentioned earlier (that can be used anywhere by anyone eg gitlab-ci)
  • Monitoring of deployed stuff (need to check mpl and mpl api to maybe add it myself to capic)
  • Cloud deployable and multi-user (for example, to have ui for Event mesh amqp/http client there)
  • Usable by business users to produce test data (also maybe in cloud so that they don't have to deal with local setup)
  • Framework/guidelines for designing testable iflows (so that it is, well, you know, at least no that bad)
Business Model

Well, again, investing in such product would bring indirect return of investment in a form of competitive advantage - basically, it is selling quality to those who is willing to buy it.

Also if it saves time of your devs and they deal with less issues during the whole project lifecycle - your price can be lower (or margin is higher).

Team / Advantage

I put it into one bullet because as I said earlier, it felt to me that CAP java could be a really nice stack for such tool.

Therefore the advantage would be to develop something first using the right technology stack aligned with sap strategy, and also that fits well in Camel/Java pro-code world.

Therefore here I am referring to the team that would make this unfair advantage happen, consisting of couple of java devs (cap java and camel/osgi experienced) + maybe one fiori frontend guy (for proper ui5 development).

Product owner with knowledge of fiori ux + cap and java + osgi + camel is essential of course.

Basically, thats it.

And based on my experience, in like 6 months such team could deliver something really nice (I guess it took me around 100-150 hrs to develop current version, though I did not track all the time, especially weekends) 

 

Call for feedback

If somehow you saw this blog before I wrote second half, please kindly provide some feedback regarding tooling you currently use and would love to use for CPI development.

entry.title: Is there a way to set initial values at go-live on entry dates on job information?
entry.content: Since Centralized Services has taken over the population of job entry/position entry dates,etc. calculations there's been an issue I haven't been able to get past. At go-live most companies will not have history to the extent necessary to have valid entry dates.  Is there a workaround I'm not aware of for this? If not, this seems like a pretty major flaw.

entry.title: Re: MRP run not to consider batches with expiry date
entry.content:
Hello @rammeshokumar 

SAP standard MRP works on material number level. It doesn't go down to batch level. Thus, your requirement can't be implement in standard. I guess you could use MRP BAdIs and try to manipulate stock levels during MRP planning. It might work, however I don't think it's the way to go.

First, do you really want to remove all the batches expiring in 6 months from MRP planning? What if you have a requirement today, would you not want to use one of the batches to cover this requirement?

If you really must not use any of the batches expiring in 6 months, why don't you just move them to blocked stock?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Bank Statement BY API OF SAP BUSINESS BYDESIGN managebankstatementin
entry.content:
Hi im using the managebankstatementin to create bank statment, With money outflow movements I can make payments that are already in the system be offset with Manual Payment, Cash Transfer etc example ws

 

<Item ActionCode="01">
<!--Consecutivo:-->
<ItemID>2</ItemID>
<BusinessPartnerInternalID>P0000029</BusinessPartnerInternalID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ItemDocumentDescription>ITEM2</ItemDocumentDescription>
<!--Optional:-->
<PaymentReference>
<ID>192</ID>
<!--Optional:-->
</PaymentReference>
<BusinessProcessVariantTypeCode>42</BusinessProcessVariantTypeCode>
<PaymentTransactionCategoryCode>1</PaymentTransactionCategoryCode>
<!--Optional:-->
<PostedAmount currencyCode="MXN">-1122.50</PostedAmount>
<!--Optional:-->
<BankValueDate>2025-03-02</BankValueDate>
<!--Optional:-->
<BankPostingDate>2025-03-02</BankPostingDate>
<OriginalCurrencyAmount currencyCode="MXN">-1122.50</OriginalCurrencyAmount>
<PaymentExplanationNote ActionCode="01">
<!--Optional:-->
<OrdinalNumberValue>1</OrdinalNumberValue>
<!--Optional:-->
<Note languageCode="ES">sdsfs9</Note>
</PaymentExplanationNote>
</Item>

 

is very simple, because only with the <PaymentReference> <ID>192</ID> (number of payment transfer), BankValueDate,  PostedAmount and BusinessPartnerInternalID, The system does a lot with the Manual Payment on cash transfer already created in SAP, obviously before doing that I extracted the SAP information from the payments and obtained the supplier ID and the payment ID in SAP very very nice buttt

 

I HAVA PROBLEM WITH "Payment Advice" 

<Item ActionCode="01">
<!--Consecutivo:-->
<ItemID>1</ItemID>
<BusinessPartnerInternalID>C0004324</BusinessPartnerInternalID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ItemDocumentDescription>ITEM1</ItemDocumentDescription>
<!--Optional:-->
<PaymentReference>
<ID>16</ID>
<!--Optional:-->
</PaymentReference>
<BusinessProcessVariantTypeCode>48</BusinessProcessVariantTypeCode>
<PaymentTransactionCategoryCode>15</PaymentTransactionCategoryCode>
<!--Optional:-->
<PostedAmount currencyCode="MXN">1000.00</PostedAmount>
<!--Optional:-->
<BankValueDate>2025-02-27</BankValueDate>
<!--Optional:-->
<BankPostingDate>2025-02-27</BankPostingDate>
<OriginalCurrencyAmount currencyCode="MXN">1000.00</OriginalCurrencyAmount>
<PaymentExplanationNote ActionCode="01">
<!--Optional:-->
<OrdinalNumberValue>1</OrdinalNumberValue>
<!--Optional:-->
<Note languageCode="ES">Confirmar pago ref.5484512151</Note>
</PaymentExplanationNote>
</Item>

I PUT THE SAME REFERENCE BUT OF THE MONEY INPUT BUT THE SYSTEM DOES NOT RELATE IT TO A PAYMENT THAT WE RECEIVED FROM A CLIENT THAT WE ALREADY CREATED IN THE SYSTEM AS "Payment Advice", it creates a payment on account in favor of the client but I already have it in the system I just need it related to the payment advice that the system already has but I don't know how I have already tried until with "Rules for Analyzing Memo Lines"

I made the same example as SAP is their documentation.

Find File References Contained in Memo Line Instead of Dedicated Bank Statement Field

Example Memo Line:

Confirm Paym. Ref. 5484512151

 

Explanation:

The memo line contains a reference to a payment confirmation (such as a confirmation of an outgoing bank transfer, a check deposit, or an outgoing check), which is usually entered by the bank in a dedicated field of the bank statement file.

With the rule you create, you can find references based on the memo line. Using these references, the system tries to find the corresponding payment or payment message in order to confirm the bank statement.

Create the rule with the following parameters:

Column or Field Name

Selection or User Entry

Rule Definition Mode

Search Term

Rule Type

DME Reference

Text before Search Result

Confirm Paym. Ref.

Minimum Length

3

Maximum Length

32

 but i didn´'t work, is it possible  with Payment Advice??
 
because i now if you put the invoice ect ect it woks well but i want to Payment Advice"

entry.title: Re: getting content type error when make remote service call
entry.content: issues resolved, destination was not set up correctly

entry.title: Data Action SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a dimension in SAP that shows the adjustments that a person can receive. In the calculation, I need to consider that if one adjustment comes before the other, it will be considered in the adjustment below it: For example: adjustment 1 in January, adjustment 2 in January, adjustment 3 in November, adjustment 4 in April. Adjustment 4 needs to be considered first in this case, so the calculation for adjustment 3 would be adjustment 1 + 2 + 4, but if it were adjustment 4 after adjustment 3, it would be adjustment 1 + 2, and adjustment 4 would take adjustments 1, 2 and 3. In SAC, I'm trying to do it using the null value with date = previous, but it gives a scope error, even though I'm putting in all the dimensions I use. Is there another way instead of using null in SAC that could have the same effect?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Available Material SAP 4/HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Thank you - but ideally would like to get a report (if not a custom CDS) to see "available" stock

entry.title: Re: Available Material SAP 4/HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: MD04 doesn't show available stock!

entry.title: Re: Available Material SAP 4/HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @AdamHorne 

You can read material availability with the BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY function. I think that the best you can get. The function reads ATP information for a single material only.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: EC Mini Master - Create custom MDF and portlets, EC Alerts
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone advise when implementing Onboarding 2.0 with EC Mini-Master, can we create custom MDF objects/portlets and new hire template for the Personal Data Collection step?

And can we use Document Templates + EC Alerts to send out email notifications? The background is we are transforming from Onboarding 1.0 to 2.0. There are a couple of custom panels and notifications in 1.0 that we want to move to 2.0. The feasible solutions seem to be using custom objects for the panels, and document template + EC alert to send out notifications post day 1.

Thank you.
